reasoning: Simple all-years average (2.4979) with modest downward adjustment. Despite high CV (25%), the data is voluminous and well-distributed across 24 accident years. The slope shows directional noise (0.63 to 0.11 across time horizons) but no consistent trend, suggesting the average adequately captures underlying pattern. Weighted average (2.4566) and exclude-high-low (2.4952) cluster tightly around the simple average. Selection of 2.45 reflects confidence in the central estimate while acknowledging the inherent volatility in early reporting periods.

Data richness (22 observations) supports average selection. 1.34 is defensible without need for more caution.
